document.name: gan-harness-glm-visual-dashboard
document.title: GAN visual loop (GLM-5.2 on JuiceFS): standalone workflow-builder dashboard, browser-critic graded
document.summary: GLM-5.2 (dapr-agent-py-juicefs) plans + builds a standalone SvelteKit dashboard aggregating workflow-builder primitives (workflows/runs, sessions, fleet capacity, gitops pipeline) from mock data, graded by a real-browser visual critic (cli-playwright-critic-agent) that screenshots the rendered app. Defaults: plan/generate=GLM-5.2, critic=Playwright; evaluationProfile=ui-web, designPass=true. All agents share the per-execution JuiceFS /sandbox/work.

intent.title: Redesign request
intent.default: Build a self-contained, polished operations dashboard for the workflow-builder platform — no backend; ALL data comes from mock modules under src/lib/mock/. Aggregate FOUR primitive groups in one cohesive layout: (1) Workflows & runs — a recent-runs list with status, duration and a working status filter, plus headline stats (active sessions, recent changes); (2) Sessions — a table of agent sessions showing status, tokens in/out, cost, and context-window % with a visible warning state when context exceeds ~80%; (3) Fleet capacity — headroom, pending admissions, an admission-latency figure, and the contributor/runtime mix; (4) GitOps pipeline — the commit->build->deploy stages with per-stage health badges (Ready/Healthy/Degraded). Provide working navigation between the four sections and render sensible empty/loading states. The app MUST build and preview cleanly (adapter-static + prerender).
intent.description: What the agents change in the repo.

planAgent.title: Planner agent
planAgent.description: PLAN phase agent (clones + writes SPEC). Same workspace-backend family.

criticAgent.title: Critic agent
criticAgent.description: EVALUATOR agent — negotiates the contract AND grades the build (drives Playwright MCP).

buildCommand.title: Build command
buildCommand.description: Build command the deterministic gate runs ('auto' = detect pnpm/npm/cargo/make).

previewCommand.title: Preview command
previewCommand.description: Preview/serve command the critic views ('auto' = detect npm/pnpm).

designPass.title: Two-pass design
designPass.description: Run the design-tokens+wireframe review BEFORE code ('false' to skip).

criticVotes.title: Critic votes
criticVotes.description: Independent Evaluator votes per build iteration; a criterion passes only if ALL votes agree (1 = single critic).

maxRestarts.title: Max restarts
maxRestarts.description: How many times the Evaluator may force a from-scratch restart (git reset --hard baseline) on fundamentally-broken work.

repoUrl.title: Repository (owner/repo)
repoUrl.description: GitHub owner/repo the agents clone + (optionally) open a PR into.

repoRef.title: Branch
repoRef.description: Branch to clone from and use as the PR base.

evaluationProfile.title: Evaluation profile
evaluationProfile.description: How the Evaluator grounds + grades: 'ui-web' (Playwright + design rubric), 'library' (run tests + exercise the public API), 'service' (boot + hit endpoints + integration tests).

outputMode.title: Output mode
outputMode.description: Remote-write policy: 'pr' (push branch + open PR), 'branch' (push branch only), 'none' (no remote change).

testCommand.title: Test command
testCommand.description: Objective verify the gate runs for code profiles ('auto' = detect npm/pnpm test, cargo test, pytest).

taskScope.title: Task scope
taskScope.default: Edit only files under src/ (routes, components, and mock data in src/lib/mock/*.ts). Keep adapter-static with prerender=true so the app renders fully standalone with no backend or network calls. Do NOT add heavy dependencies, a database, or server routes. Keep `npm run build` and `npm run preview` working.
taskScope.description: Free-form hint of what/where to change (replaces UI-file hardcoding).

intent.title: Redesign request

with.command:
${ "set -e
cd /sandbox/work
rm -rf repo
REF=" + ((.trigger.repoRef // "") | @sh) + "
BF=""; [ -n "$REF" ] && BF="--branch $REF"
git clone --depth 1 --single-branch $BF https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/" + (.trigger.repoUrl // "PittampalliOrg/glm-dashboard-starter") + ".git repo
cd /sandbox/work/repo
git config user.email agent@workflow-builder.local
git config user.name "workflow-builder agent"
echo CLONED $(git rev-parse HEAD) on $(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)" }

agentConfig.name: Planner
agentConfig.instructions:
BUILD/INSTALL LOCATION (CRITICAL): /sandbox/work is a SLOW NETWORK filesystem (JuiceFS) — running `npm install`/`npm ci`/build there FAILS with ENOTEMPTY rename errors and 300s+ timeouts for non-trivial dependency trees. NEVER run npm install or a build in /sandbox/work/repo. Make your SOURCE EDITS in /sandbox/work/repo (durable, shared with the gate/critic), but run ALL install/build/test in a LOCAL copy on fast disk: `mkdir -p /sandbox/scratch/repo && export npm_config_cache=/sandbox/scratch/.npm && tar -C /sandbox/work/repo --exclude=node_modules --exclude=.svelte-kit --exclude=.next --exclude=build --exclude=dist --exclude=.git -cf - . | tar -C /sandbox/scratch/repo -xf - && cd /sandbox/scratch/repo && npm install --no-audit --no-fund` then build/test there. Re-sync (re-run the tar) after each round of edits before re-building. node_modules/build artifacts must NEVER be written to /sandbox/work/repo.

You are the PLANNER for an autonomous coding task. The target repo is ALREADY cloned at /sandbox/work/repo. READ the repo (entry points, package manifest, existing tests) and write a HIGH-LEVEL spec to /sandbox/work/SPEC.md (ONLY that file; do NOT edit the repo). Cover: the product/task context, the target user/consumer, and the quality dimensions that matter for THIS task — for a library/service task: correctness, tests, code_quality, maintainability, performance. Deliberately stay HIGH-LEVEL: do NOT enumerate granular, implementation-level acceptance criteria — the Generator and the Evaluator negotiate the specific, testable criteria next. End with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Task:
" + (.trigger.intent // "make the requested change") + "

Scope hint: " + (.trigger.taskScope // "") + "

Target repo: https://github.com/" + (.trigger.repoUrl // "PittampalliOrg/glm-dashboard-starter") + " (branch " + (.trigger.repoRef // "master") + ")" + "
The repo is ALREADY cloned at /sandbox/work/repo. READ it (entry points, package manifest, existing tests) and write a HIGH-LEVEL spec to /sandbox/work/SPEC.md (product/task context + the quality dimensions relevant to this task). Do NOT enumerate granular acceptance criteria (those are negotiated next). Do NOT edit the repo. End with a one-line summary." }

with.command:
${ "export WFB_EVAL_PROFILE=" + ((.trigger.evaluationProfile // "ui-web") | @sh) + "; export WFB_REPO=" + ((.trigger.repoUrl // "PittampalliOrg/glm-dashboard-starter") | @sh) + "; export WFB_BASE=" + ((.trigger.repoRef // "main") | @sh) + "; export WFB_OUT=" + ((.trigger.outputMode // "pr") | @sh) + "; export WFB_INTENT=" + ((.trigger.intent // "automated change") | @sh) + "; export WFB_BUILD_OVERRIDE=" + ((.trigger.buildCommand // "") | @sh) + "; export WFB_INSTALL_OVERRIDE=" + ((.trigger.installCommand // "") | @sh) + "; export WFB_PREVIEW_OVERRIDE=" + ((.trigger.previewCommand // "") | @sh) + "; " + "WFB_INSTALL="$WFB_INSTALL_OVERRIDE"; WFB_BUILD="$WFB_BUILD_OVERRIDE"; WFB_PREVIEW="$WFB_PREVIEW_OVERRIDE"
cd /sandbox/work/repo 2>/dev/null || true
# W1: build in a LOCAL copy — JuiceFS small-file I/O makes npm install ~11min vs ~4s on local.
# Source-of-truth stays on /sandbox/work/repo (agent edits + diff + Files tab); node_modules/build never touch JuiceFS.
WFB_WORKREPO=/sandbox/work/repo; WFB_BUILDREPO=/sandbox/scratch/repo
mkdir -p $WFB_BUILDREPO /sandbox/scratch/.npm
export npm_config_cache=/sandbox/scratch/.npm; export TMPDIR=/sandbox/scratch/tmp
tar -C $WFB_WORKREPO --exclude=node_modules --exclude=.svelte-kit --exclude=build --exclude=.git -cf - . 2>/dev/null | tar -C $WFB_BUILDREPO -xf - 2>/dev/null || true
cd $WFB_BUILDREPO 2>/dev/null || true
if [ -z "$WFB_INSTALL" ] || [ "$WFB_INSTALL" = auto ]; then
  if [ -f pnpm-lock.yaml ]; then WFB_INSTALL='pnpm install --frozen-lockfile';
  elif [ -f package.json ]; then WFB_INSTALL='npm install --no-audit --no-fund';
  elif [ -f Cargo.toml ]; then WFB_INSTALL='cargo fetch';
  elif [ -f requirements.txt ]; then WFB_INSTALL='pip install -r requirements.txt';
  else WFB_INSTALL='true'; fi
fi
if [ -z "$WFB_BUILD" ] || [ "$WFB_BUILD" = auto ]; then
  if [ -f pnpm-lock.yaml ]; then WFB_BUILD='pnpm build';
  elif [ -f package.json ]; then WFB_BUILD='npm run build';
  elif [ -f Cargo.toml ]; then WFB_BUILD='cargo build --release';
  elif [ -f Makefile ] && grep -q '^build:' Makefile; then WFB_BUILD='make build';
  else WFB_BUILD='echo no-build-step'; fi
fi
if [ -z "$WFB_PREVIEW" ] || [ "$WFB_PREVIEW" = auto ]; then
  if [ -f package.json ]; then WFB_PREVIEW='npm run preview -- --host 127.0.0.1';
  else WFB_PREVIEW='true'; fi
fi
sh -c "$WFB_INSTALL" >/tmp/install.log 2>&1 || true
printf '%s' "$WFB_EVAL_PROFILE" > /sandbox/work/.wfb_profile
printf '%s' "$WFB_REPO" > /sandbox/work/.wfb_repo
printf '%s' "$WFB_BASE" > /sandbox/work/.wfb_base
printf '%s' "$WFB_OUT" > /sandbox/work/.wfb_out
printf '%s' "$WFB_INTENT" > /sandbox/work/.wfb_intent
BASE=$(git rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo unknown); printf '{"baseline":"%s","log":[]}' "$BASE" > /sandbox/work/progress.json; cat /sandbox/work/progress.json" }

design.if: ${ (.approve_goal_spec.timedOut // false) == false and (.approve_goal_spec.approved // true) == true and ((.trigger.designPass // "true") != "false") and ((.trigger.evaluationProfile // "ui-web") == "ui-web") }

agentConfig.name: Designer (Generator, two-pass)
agentConfig.instructions:
You are the GENERATOR doing the FIRST design pass, BEFORE writing any code (no CSS/HTML yet). Read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md. Produce a compact, OPINIONATED design system and commit to a distinctive direction (avoid generic 'AI slop' — no default purple-gradient-on-white-cards, no near-black + acid-green). Write TWO files at /sandbox/work (NOT in the repo):
1) /sandbox/work/design-tokens.json — STRICT JSON: {"palette":[{"name":"...","hex":"#..."}, ... 4-6 named colors], "typography":{"display":"font + role","body":"font + role"}, "spacing":"the scale/rhythm", "motif":"the one bold, justifiable aesthetic risk"}.
2) /sandbox/work/wireframe.txt — an ASCII wireframe of the page layout (hero, feature cards, pricing, CTA), top to bottom.
On later rounds READ /sandbox/work/design-review.json (the Evaluator's objections) and REVISE both files to address every objection. Do NOT edit the repo. End with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Two-pass design (proposal). Intent: " + (.trigger.intent // "redesign the landing page") + "

Read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md and, if present, /sandbox/work/design-review.json (prior objections). Write/revise /sandbox/work/design-tokens.json (4-6 named hex + display/body type + spacing + one bold motif) and /sandbox/work/wireframe.txt (ASCII layout). No code." }

agentConfig.name: Design lead (Evaluator, two-pass)
agentConfig.instructions: You are a SKEPTICAL design lead at a small studio reviewing a design PLAN before any code (do NOT open a browser; there is nothing to view yet). Read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md, /sandbox/work/design-tokens.json and /sandbox/work/wireframe.txt. Judge ONLY on Originality and Craft: does the palette/typography/spacing form a coherent, DISTINCTIVE identity grounded in the product+audience, with at least one bold, justifiable aesthetic risk? REJECT generic library-default / 'AI slop' choices (ubiquitous purple gradients over white cards; near-black backgrounds with acid-green accents; default system fonts with no hierarchy). Write /sandbox/work/design-review.json as STRICT JSON: {"approved": <bool>, "feedback": "specific, actionable objections or what to change"}. Set approved:true ONLY when the direction is distinctive and coherent (not generic) — otherwise approved:false with concrete objections. Judge by what a viewer will SEE, not by prescribed CSS values. End with a one-line summary.

body.prompt: ${ "Review the DESIGN PLAN (no code yet). Read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md, design-tokens.json, wireframe.txt. Write /sandbox/work/design-review.json {approved, feedback}. Reject generic/AI-slop direction; approve only a distinctive, coherent one." }

with.command:
for i in $(seq 1 30); do [ -s /sandbox/work/design-review.json ] && break; sleep 1; done
python3 - <<'PYZZ'
import json,os
P='/sandbox/work/design-review.json'
try:
    d=json.load(open(P))
except Exception:
    print(json.dumps({'approved':False,'haveTokens':os.path.exists('/sandbox/work/design-tokens.json'),'error':'design-review.json missing/invalid'}));raise SystemExit
ap=d.get('approved')
if ap is None:
    txt=str(d.get('verdict') or d.get('overall_verdict') or d.get('summary') or d.get('feedback') or '').lower()
    ap=(('approv' in txt) or ('looks good' in txt) or ('lgtm' in txt) or ('accept' in txt)) and not (('reject' in txt) or ('revise' in txt) or ('not approv' in txt))
have=os.path.exists('/sandbox/work/design-tokens.json')
approved=bool(ap) and have
print(json.dumps({'approved':approved,'haveTokens':have}))
PYZZ

agentConfig.name: Proposer (Generator)
agentConfig.instructions: You are the GENERATOR in a contract NEGOTIATION, BEFORE writing any code. Read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md. Propose a concrete plan: break the work into a SMALL set of discrete, INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIABLE changes. For EACH, state (a) what you will implement, (b) EXACTLY how it can be verified — using the verification method the prompt specifies for this task's evaluation profile (a browser interaction for UI work; a shell/test command or function-call assertion for code work), and (c) a crisp acceptance criterion. On later rounds, READ /sandbox/work/contract-review.md (the Evaluator's objections) and REVISE your proposal to address every objection. Write your proposal to /sandbox/work/proposal.md (ONLY that file; do NOT edit the repo and do NOT write contract.json). End with a one-line summary. Do NOT run npm install / npm run build|test|check / npx vitest / tsc or any build in /sandbox/work/repo - it is a slow network filesystem and you do not need to; inspect the source STATICALLY to propose/review the criteria (the deterministic gate runs the build + tests later in a fast local copy).

body.prompt:
${ "Contract negotiation (proposal). Intent: " + (.trigger.intent // "make the requested change") + "
Scope: " + (.trigger.taskScope // "") + "
Evaluation profile: " + (.trigger.evaluationProfile // "ui-web") + "

The code under test is in /sandbox/work/repo — inspect source files THERE (e.g. /sandbox/work/repo/index.js, /sandbox/work/repo/test.js, /sandbox/work/repo/package.json), NOT at the /sandbox/work root. Read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md and, if it exists, /sandbox/work/contract-review.md (prior objections). Keep tool use MINIMAL — read what you need, do not exhaustively execute the code. Write/revise /sandbox/work/proposal.md with discrete changes, each with an acceptance criterion that is " + (if (.trigger.evaluationProfile // "ui-web") == "ui-web" then "BROWSER-verifiable (what a design reviewer sees and clicks on the rendered page)" else "COMMAND/TEST-verifiable — name the EXACT shell/test command or function-call assertion that proves it (e.g. `npm test` passes, `python -m pytest -q` passes, import the module and assert f(x)==expected, or curl an endpoint and assert the status). NO browser checks." end) + ". CRITERIA RULE: every acceptance criterion must state OBSERVABLE BEHAVIOR (what becomes true or visible to a user), NOT a code prescription — never say add this exact line/function to a specific file; the Generator chooses the implementation." }

agentConfig.name: Evaluator (Contract negotiation)
agentConfig.instructions: You are a HIGHLY SKEPTICAL EVALUATOR negotiating the acceptance contract for a coding task, BEFORE any code is written. You do NOT write code and you do NOT open a browser yet. Read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md and /sandbox/work/proposal.md. Adversarially review the proposed features + acceptance criteria. Reject vague/unmeasurable/overly-broad criteria and ones that omit key edge cases (empty/error states, responsive/mobile layout, keyboard focus / accessibility, interactive elements actually working). Produce a FOCUSED contract — aim for roughly 8-15 of the MOST IMPORTANT, user-visible acceptance criteria (quality over quantity; don't pad with dozens of micro-checks, but don't drop important coverage either). Prefer criteria checkable by THIS task's verification method (the prompt states it): for UI work, perceptual/structural checks a design reviewer makes by looking at and clicking the rendered page (e.g. 'the hero headline is clearly the largest text', 'the primary CTA is visually distinct'); for code work, criteria whose `verify` names the EXACT shell/test command or function-call assertion that proves them (e.g. '`npm test` passes', 'import the module and assert f(x)==expected', 'curl /health → 200') — NOT browser checks. AVOID brittle exact-number assertions (specific pixel sizes / hex / rgb values) where a perceptual description works; describe the INTENT. Write your objections + required changes to /sandbox/work/contract-review.md. THEN write the negotiated contract to /sandbox/work/contract.json as JSON of the form {"agreed": <boolean>, "criteria": [{"id":"c1","kind":"objective|subjective","dimension":"<task dimension>","description":"the criterion","verify":"EXACTLY how to check it (a browser interaction for UI; a shell/test command for code)","passes": false}, ...]}. Mark each criterion kind=objective if it is verifiable by running a command/test (it MUST name the command in verify), or kind=subjective if it needs judgment against a rubric. Use task-appropriate dimensions: for UI use design_quality|originality|craft|functionality; for a library/service use correctness|tests|code_quality|maintainability|performance. Give each criterion a clear description + verify and a dimension when you can; every criterion starts with "passes": false. (A deterministic normalizer canonicalizes field names afterward, so focus on clear, testable description+verify rather than exact key names.) CONVERGENCE: set "agreed": true as soon as the criteria are specific, verifiable by the task's method, and cover the key cases — do NOT withhold agreement over minor polish; the build loop iterates on quality. Only keep "agreed": false if criteria are genuinely vague or missing important coverage. REJECT any criterion phrased as a code prescription (a specific line/function/file to add); rewrite it as observable behavior whose verify names the proving command or interaction. End with a one-line summary. Do NOT run npm install / npm run build|test|check / npx vitest / tsc or any build in /sandbox/work/repo - it is a slow network filesystem and you do not need to; inspect the source STATICALLY to propose/review the criteria (the deterministic gate runs the build + tests later in a fast local copy).

body.prompt: ${ "Negotiate the acceptance contract for the '" + (.trigger.evaluationProfile // "ui-web") + "' profile. The code under test is in /sandbox/work/repo — inspect source files THERE (e.g. /sandbox/work/repo/index.js, /sandbox/work/repo/test.js), NOT at the /sandbox/work root. Read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md and /sandbox/work/proposal.md, write your objections to /sandbox/work/contract-review.md, and write/update /sandbox/work/contract.json (agreed + criteria, each kind/dimension/verify, passes:false) as STRICT JSON per your instructions. Each criterion must be " + (if (.trigger.evaluationProfile // "ui-web") == "ui-web" then "BROWSER-verifiable (what a design reviewer sees and clicks on the rendered page)" else "COMMAND/TEST-verifiable — name the EXACT shell/test command or function-call assertion that proves it (e.g. `npm test` passes, `python -m pytest -q` passes, import the module and assert f(x)==expected, or curl an endpoint and assert the status). NO browser checks." end) + " Be decisive and EFFICIENT: judge criteria by READING the source + proposal — do NOT execute the code to verify. Use at most a few tool calls, then write the two files and STOP. Set agreed:true as soon as the criteria are specific and verifiable by the stated method; they need not be exhaustive." }

with.command:
for i in $(seq 1 30); do [ -s /sandbox/work/contract.json ] && break; sleep 1; done
python3 - <<'PYZZ'
import json,re,os
P='/sandbox/work/contract.json'
_UIDIMS=('design_quality','originality','craft','functionality')
_CODEDIMS=('correctness','tests','code_quality','maintainability','performance')
_ALLDIMS=_UIDIMS+_CODEDIMS
def dim(t):
    t=t.lower()
    if re.search(r'test|spec|coverage|assert|pytest|exit code',t): return 'tests'
    if re.search(r'perf|latency|throughput|benchmark|memory|speed',t): return 'performance'
    if re.search(r'lint|format|typecheck|type-check|readab|docstring|comment',t): return 'code_quality'
    if re.search(r'maintain|modular|coupl|cohes|structure|duplicat',t): return 'maintainability'
    if re.search(r'typograph|spacing|color|contrast|hierarch|whitespace|align|legib',t): return 'craft'
    if re.search(r'distinct|original|custom|bold|unique|generic|template|slop|memorable',t): return 'originality'
    if re.search(r'identity|mood|cohesi|brand|aesthetic|palette|tone|polish',t): return 'design_quality'
    if re.search(r'click|render|responsive|keyboard|accessib|hover|focus|navigat|button|layout|visible',t): return 'functionality'
    return 'correctness'
def kindof(desc,ver):
    t=(str(desc)+' '+str(ver)).lower()
    return 'objective' if re.search(r'\btest\b|command|exit|run |npm |pnpm |cargo |pytest|curl|build|lint|typecheck|assert|returns?\b|==',t) else 'subjective'
try:
    raw=json.load(open(P))
except Exception:
    print(json.dumps({'agreed':False,'criteriaCount':0,'valid':False,'error':'contract.json missing/invalid'}));raise SystemExit
crit=raw.get('criteria') if isinstance(raw.get('criteria'),list) else []
norm=[]
for i,c in enumerate(crit):
    if not isinstance(c,dict): continue
    desc=c.get('description') or c.get('text') or c.get('criterion') or c.get('requirement') or c.get('name') or ''
    ver=c.get('verify') or c.get('verification') or c.get('check') or c.get('how') or ''
    dd=c.get('dimension')
    if dd not in _ALLDIMS: dd=dim(str(desc)+' '+str(ver))
    kk=c.get('kind') if c.get('kind') in ('objective','subjective') else kindof(desc,ver)
    norm.append({'id':str(c.get('id') or ('c'+str(i+1))),'kind':kk,'dimension':dd,'description':str(desc),'verify':str(ver),'passes':bool(c.get('passes'))})
def _nk(s): return ' '.join(re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9 ]',' ',str(s).lower()).split())
seen=set(); dd2=[]
for c in norm:
    k=_nk(c['description'])
    if k and k in seen: continue
    if k: seen.add(k)
    dd2.append(c)
removed=len(norm)-len(dd2); norm=dd2
_MAXC=int(os.environ.get('WFB_MAX_CRITERIA','24') or 24)
capped=max(0,len(norm)-_MAXC); norm=norm[:_MAXC]
valid=len(norm)>=3 and all(x['description'] for x in norm)
agreed=bool(raw.get('agreed')) and valid
raw['criteria']=norm; raw['agreed']=agreed
json.dump(raw,open(P,'w'))
print(json.dumps({'agreed':agreed,'criteriaCount':len(norm),'deduped':removed,'capped':capped,'valid':valid}))
PYZZ

refine.while: ${ ( (( ((.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") | test("OBJECTIVE PASS")) and (((((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) | (.meets_criteria // false)) == true) ) | not) and ((((((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) | (.stalled // false)) == true) | not) ) }

agentConfig.name: Generator
agentConfig.instructions:
BUILD/INSTALL LOCATION (CRITICAL): /sandbox/work is a SLOW NETWORK filesystem (JuiceFS) — running `npm install`/`npm ci`/build there FAILS with ENOTEMPTY rename errors and 300s+ timeouts for non-trivial dependency trees. NEVER run npm install or a build in /sandbox/work/repo. Make your SOURCE EDITS in /sandbox/work/repo (durable, shared with the gate/critic), but run ALL install/build/test in a LOCAL copy on fast disk: `mkdir -p /sandbox/scratch/repo && export npm_config_cache=/sandbox/scratch/.npm && tar -C /sandbox/work/repo --exclude=node_modules --exclude=.svelte-kit --exclude=.next --exclude=build --exclude=dist --exclude=.git -cf - . | tar -C /sandbox/scratch/repo -xf - && cd /sandbox/scratch/repo && npm install --no-audit --no-fund` then build/test there. Re-sync (re-run the tar) after each round of edits before re-building. node_modules/build artifacts must NEVER be written to /sandbox/work/repo.

You are the GENERATOR. Implement the redesign in /sandbox/work/repo to satisfy the NEGOTIATED CONTRACT. GETTING UP TO SPEED (do this FIRST, every turn): read /sandbox/work/progress.json (the chronological log of prior iterations) and run `git -C /sandbox/work/repo log --oneline -10`. If /sandbox/work/design-tokens.json and /sandbox/work/wireframe.txt exist, treat them as the AUTHORITATIVE visual direction (color tokens, typography, spacing, the bold motif, layout) — implement THAT design; do not invent a different palette/type system. Then read /sandbox/work/contract.json (the AUTHORITATIVE acceptance criteria — each has id/description/verify/passes) and /sandbox/work/SPEC.md. Focus on the criteria whose "passes" is false. Edit whatever files the task requires (see the task scope in the prompt). Prefer the smallest change that satisfies the FAILING criteria; do NOT add dependencies unless the task explicitly calls for it. You do NOT grade your own work: a deterministic build gate + an independent skeptical Evaluator (who RUNS/EXERCISES the artifact per the evaluation profile and grades EACH contract criterion) evaluate every attempt. Each turn: address the build output AND the Evaluator's per-criterion feedback, make the most impactful improvement toward the FAILING criteria, dependencies are PRE-INSTALLED (node_modules present) so run the project's build yourself to verify before finishing, end with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Task: " + (.trigger.intent // "make the requested change") + "
Scope: " + (.trigger.taskScope // "") + "

Repo: /sandbox/work/repo. Authoritative acceptance criteria: /sandbox/work/contract.json. Spec: /sandbox/work/SPEC.md. Memory: /sandbox/work/progress.json (read it + git log FIRST)." + "

Your previous attempt:
" + (.loop.last.generate.content // "(first attempt)") + "

Build gate (must end OBJECTIVE PASS):
" + ((.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") // "(not run yet)") + "

Evaluator per-criterion feedback (from the browser):
" + (try ((((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) | (.feedback // .summary // "(none)")) catch "(none)") + "

Failing criteria (fix each specifically):
" + (try ((((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) | (.failing // []) | map("- [" + (.id|tostring) + "] " + (.description // "") + (if (.output // "") != "" then " (observed: " + .output + ")" else "" end)) | join("
")) catch "") + "

IMPORTANT: a fast deterministic gate installs + builds the project for you on local disk and reports OBJECTIVE PASS — do NOT run ANY install/build/test command in /sandbox/work/repo - NO npm install, npm run build, npm run check, npm test, npx vitest, or tsc (/sandbox/work is a network filesystem where these are extremely slow, hit cross-user permission errors on node_modules, and will stall your turn; a fast deterministic gate already installs + builds + tests your changes in a LOCAL copy and reports OBJECTIVE PASS). Do NOT copy the repo elsewhere to build - just WRITE source files. ONLY edit source files to satisfy every FAILING contract criterion. CRITICAL: creating component or module files is NOT enough — the app ENTRY POINT (e.g. src/routes/+page.svelte) MUST import and RENDER them so the running app actually shows your work; if the evaluator reports the app shows only the original scaffold or placeholder, your entry point is not wired — fix the entry point FIRST. Then end with a brief summary." }

agentConfig.name: Critic
agentConfig.instructions:
BUILD/INSTALL LOCATION (CRITICAL): /sandbox/work is a SLOW NETWORK filesystem (JuiceFS) — running `npm install`/`npm ci`/build there FAILS with ENOTEMPTY rename errors and 300s+ timeouts for non-trivial dependency trees. NEVER run npm install or a build in /sandbox/work/repo. Make your SOURCE EDITS in /sandbox/work/repo (durable, shared with the gate/critic), but run ALL install/build/test in a LOCAL copy on fast disk: `mkdir -p /sandbox/scratch/repo && export npm_config_cache=/sandbox/scratch/.npm && tar -C /sandbox/work/repo --exclude=node_modules --exclude=.svelte-kit --exclude=.next --exclude=build --exclude=dist --exclude=.git -cf - . | tar -C /sandbox/scratch/repo -xf - && cd /sandbox/scratch/repo && npm install --no-audit --no-fund` then build/test there. Re-sync (re-run the tar) after each round of edits before re-building. node_modules/build artifacts must NEVER be written to /sandbox/work/repo.

You are an exacting, SKEPTICAL EVALUATOR with strong taste, independent of the builder and of any other evaluator; you do NOT edit the repo. GRADE the running app against the negotiated contract.

STEP 1 — build + start the app on LOCAL scratch (the /sandbox/work JuiceFS workspace is far too slow for npm install). Copy the source to local disk, install + build + preview there, then wait until it answers:
  mkdir -p /sandbox/scratch/repo /sandbox/scratch/.npm; export npm_config_cache=/sandbox/scratch/.npm; tar -C /sandbox/work/repo --exclude=node_modules --exclude=.svelte-kit --exclude=.git -cf - . | tar -C /sandbox/scratch/repo -xf - ; cd /sandbox/scratch/repo && npm install --no-audit --no-fund && npm run build && (nohup npm run preview -- --port 4173 --host 127.0.0.1 >/tmp/preview.log 2>&1 &) ; for i in $(seq 1 60); do curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:4173/ >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 1; done ; tail -5 /tmp/preview.log
If preview never answers, fall back to dev on the same port and wait the same way.

STEP 2 — inspect with the Playwright MCP browser tools (server 'playwright'): browser_navigate to http://127.0.0.1:4173/ , browser_snapshot, browser_take_screenshot (filename 'critic-shot.png' -> /sandbox/work/critic-shot.png). Resize the viewport (desktop/tablet/mobile) and browser_click the nav / primary CTA, re-snapshotting, to test interactivity + responsiveness. Use the BROWSER — do not write your own screenshot scripts.

IGNORE churn in package-lock.json, node_modules/, .svelte-kit/, build/. Judge ONLY the redesign source (src/) and the rendered UI.

STEP 3 — read /sandbox/work/contract.json (the AUTHORITATIVE criteria), /sandbox/work/SPEC.md, /sandbox/work/design-tokens.json (the agreed visual direction), and the source.

STEP 4 — grade the rendered app against EACH criterion. Decide passes true/false based on what you ACTUALLY SAW / INTERACTED WITH (use the criterion's "verify"). DEFAULT to passes:false unless the evidence is clear. Generic or merely 'fine' work does NOT pass.

STEP 5 — WRITE your verdict to /sandbox/work/verdict-0.json as STRICT JSON with EXACTLY this shape: {"criteria": [{"id": "c1", "passes": <bool>, "feedback": "concrete browser evidence"}, ...], "allPass": <bool>, "score": <0-100>, "meets_criteria": <same as allPass>, "recommend_restart": <bool — TRUE ONLY if the work is FUNDAMENTALLY broken (build/page does not render, core layout entirely absent) so starting over beats patching; else false>, "feedback": "overall summary of what still fails"}. Write ONLY verdict-0.json (do NOT edit contract.json — a deterministic aggregator combines the votes). CALIBRATION: mark passes:true ONLY for what you ACTUALLY SAW in a screenshot/snapshot you captured this iteration; any criterion without captured browser evidence is passes:false. After writing it, stop the server (pkill -f vite 2>/dev/null; pkill -f 'npm run' 2>/dev/null) and end with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Grade the redesigned app in /sandbox/work/repo against EACH criterion in /sandbox/work/contract.json by VIEWING + interacting with it in the browser (Playwright MCP), then WRITE /sandbox/work/verdict-0.json (per-criterion passes + allPass + meets_criteria + recommend_restart). Follow your instructions exactly." + "

Preview command for THIS run: " + (.trigger.previewCommand // "auto") }

agentConfig.name: Code critic
agentConfig.instructions:
You are an exacting, SKEPTICAL EVALUATOR independent of the builder and of any other evaluator; you do NOT edit the repo and you do NOT open a browser. GRADE the artifact in /sandbox/work/repo against the negotiated contract by RUNNING it (objective ground-truth), per the evaluation profile in the prompt.

STEP 1 — read /sandbox/work/contract.json (the AUTHORITATIVE criteria; each has id/kind/dimension/description/verify), /sandbox/work/SPEC.md, and the relevant source.

STEP 2 — GROUND IN REALITY (never grade from a static diff):
  - library profile: run the project's test suite + typecheck/lint (e.g. `npm test`, `cargo test`, `python -m pytest -q`), and EXERCISE the public API yourself in a scratch script (import it, call it with real + edge inputs, print results). Use the criterion's `verify` command where given.
  - service profile: build + START the server in the background, wait for it to listen, then HIT its endpoints with curl (status codes, happy-path + error/edge payloads) and run any integration tests. Stop the server when done.
  Capture real command output/exit codes as your evidence.

STEP 3 — grade EACH criterion. For kind=objective, it passes ONLY if the named command/test actually exits 0 / produces the asserted output (you ran it). For kind=subjective, judge against the dimension (correctness/code_quality/maintainability/performance) from what you SAW running + reading the code. DEFAULT to passes:false unless the evidence is clear; merely 'looks fine' does NOT pass.

STEP 4 — WRITE your verdict to /sandbox/work/verdict-0.json as STRICT JSON: {"criteria": [{"id": "c1", "passes": <bool>, "feedback": "concrete command output / evidence"}, ...], "allPass": <bool>, "score": <0-100>, "meets_criteria": <same as allPass>, "recommend_restart": <bool — TRUE only if fundamentally broken (does not build/import/run) so starting over beats patching; else false>, "feedback": "overall summary of what still fails"}. Write ONLY verdict-0.json (do NOT edit contract.json — a deterministic aggregator combines the votes). CALIBRATION: mark passes:true ONLY when you actually RAN the verify and pasted real output (e.g. '12 passing', exit 0); any criterion with no captured command output is passes:false. End with a one-line summary.

body.prompt: ${ "Grade the artifact in /sandbox/work/repo against EACH criterion in /sandbox/work/contract.json by RUNNING it (tests / API / endpoints) per the '" + (.trigger.evaluationProfile // "library") + "' profile, then WRITE /sandbox/work/verdict-0.json (per-criterion passes + allPass + meets_criteria + recommend_restart). Follow your instructions exactly. Test command hint: " + (.trigger.testCommand // "auto") }

with.command:
WFB_WORKREPO=/sandbox/work/repo; WFB_BUILDREPO=/sandbox/scratch/repo
mkdir -p $WFB_BUILDREPO /sandbox/scratch/.npm /sandbox/scratch/tmp
export npm_config_cache=/sandbox/scratch/.npm; export TMPDIR=/sandbox/scratch/tmp
tar -C $WFB_WORKREPO --exclude=node_modules --exclude=.svelte-kit --exclude=build --exclude=.git -cf - . 2>/dev/null | tar -C $WFB_BUILDREPO -xf - 2>/dev/null || true
cd $WFB_BUILDREPO || exit 0
[ -d node_modules ] || npm install --no-audit --no-fund >/tmp/install.log 2>&1 || true
npm run build >/tmp/build.log 2>&1 || echo BUILD_FAILED
rm -rf $WFB_WORKREPO/build 2>/dev/null
cp -r $WFB_BUILDREPO/build $WFB_WORKREPO/build 2>/dev/null && echo BUILD_PUBLISHED || echo NO-BUILD
ls -la $WFB_WORKREPO/build 2>/dev/null | head -3 || echo NO-BUILD-DIR

agentConfig.name: Final critic
agentConfig.instructions:
You are a SKEPTICAL, independent UI EVALUATOR with strong taste; you do NOT edit the repo. The app is ALREADY BUILT — a static production bundle is at /sandbox/work/repo/build. Do NOT run npm install or a build (slow on this network FS + unnecessary).

STEP 1 — serve the prebuilt static bundle locally and wait for it:
  cd /sandbox/work/repo/build && (nohup python3 -m http.server 4173 --bind 127.0.0.1 >/tmp/serve.log 2>&1 &) ; for i in $(seq 1 30); do curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:4173/ >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 1; done ; tail -3 /tmp/serve.log
If /sandbox/work/repo/build is missing or empty, say so in your verdict and set allPass false.

STEP 2 — inspect with the Playwright MCP browser tools (server 'playwright'): browser_navigate to http://127.0.0.1:4173/ , browser_snapshot, browser_take_screenshot (filename 'critic-shot.png' -> /sandbox/work/critic-shot.png). Resize the viewport (desktop/tablet/mobile) and browser_click the nav / primary CTA, re-snapshotting, to test interactivity + responsiveness. Use the BROWSER — do not write your own screenshot scripts.

STEP 3 — read /sandbox/work/contract.json (the AUTHORITATIVE criteria), /sandbox/work/SPEC.md, and /sandbox/work/design-tokens.json.

STEP 4 — grade the rendered app against EACH criterion based on what you ACTUALLY SAW / INTERACTED WITH. DEFAULT to passes:false unless the evidence is clear. Generic or merely 'fine' work does NOT pass.

STEP 5 — WRITE your verdict to /sandbox/work/verdict-final.json as STRICT JSON with EXACTLY this shape: {"criteria": [{"id": "c1", "passes": <bool>, "feedback": "concrete browser evidence"}, ...], "allPass": <bool>, "score": <0-100>, "meets_criteria": <same as allPass>, "recommend_restart": <bool>, "feedback": "overall summary"}. CALIBRATION: passes:true ONLY for what you actually saw in a screenshot/snapshot you captured. After writing it, stop the server (pkill -f http.server 2>/dev/null) and end with a one-line summary.

body.prompt: ${ "Grade the prebuilt dashboard at /sandbox/work/repo/build by VIEWING + interacting with it in the browser (Playwright MCP), then WRITE /sandbox/work/verdict-final.json. Do NOT build — the static bundle is already built. Follow your instructions exactly." }

artifacts.title: Rendered redesign (Playwright critic screenshot)
artifacts.description: Full-page screenshot the critic captured in-browser via the Playwright MCP server.

artifacts.title: Negotiated acceptance contract
artifacts.description: The Generator↔Evaluator negotiated contract.json (testable criteria + per-criterion passes flags).

with.command:
REPO=$(git -C /sandbox/work/repo remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null | sed -e 's#.*github.com[:/]##' -e 's#\.git$##'); [ -n "$REPO" ] || REPO=$(cat /sandbox/work/.wfb_repo 2>/dev/null || echo PittampalliOrg/glm-dashboard-starter)
BASE=$(cat /sandbox/work/.wfb_base 2>/dev/null || echo main)
OUT=$(cat /sandbox/work/.wfb_out 2>/dev/null || echo branch)
TITLE=$(head -c 72 /sandbox/work/.wfb_intent 2>/dev/null || echo 'automated change'); [ -n "$TITLE" ] || TITLE='automated change'
cd /sandbox/work/repo && git config user.email agent@workflow-builder.local && git config user.name 'workflow-builder agent'
BR=wfb-$(date +%s) && git checkout -b "$BR" && git add -A && (git commit -m "$TITLE (workflow-builder GAN harness)" || echo nothing-to-commit)
git push https://x-access-token:$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/$REPO.git HEAD:"$BR"
if [ "$OUT" = pr ]; then
  PR=$(curl -sS -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_TOKEN" -H 'Accept: application/vnd.github+json' https://api.github.com/repos/$REPO/pulls -d "{\"title\":\"$TITLE (automated)\",\"head\":\"$BR\",\"base\":\"$BASE\",\"body\":\"Automated change via the workflow-builder GAN coding harness (plan/negotiate/generate/evaluate).\"}")
  echo "$PR" | python3 -c 'import sys,json;d=json.load(sys.stdin);print("PR_URL="+str(d.get("html_url") or d.get("message") or d))'
else echo "BRANCH_PUSHED=$BR"; fi

set.terminalState: ${ if ( ((.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") | test("OBJECTIVE PASS")) and (((((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) | (.meets_criteria // false)) == true) ) then "satisfied" elif (((((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) | (.stalled // false)) == true) then "stalled_no_progress" else "max_iterations_reached" end }

artifacts.title: Coding redesign result
artifacts.description: Build + critic verdicts, iterations, PR output.
